useLocalStorage.title: useLocalStorage
useLocalStorage.description: Reactive ref backed by localStorage. Reads the stored value on mount, writes back on every change (deep watch for objects/arrays), and is safe to call in SSR contexts.

seo.metaDescription: useLocalStorage is a Vue 3 composable returning a reactive ref backed by localStorage: reads on mount, writes on change, deep-watches objects, SSR-safe.
seo.intro: useLocalStorage is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable that returns a reactive ref backed by localStorage — it reads the stored value on mount and writes back on every change, with deep watching for objects and SSR-safe behavior.

api.key: localStorage key used to read and write the value.
api.defaultValue: Fallback value used when the key is absent or the stored JSON cannot be parsed.
api.returns: A reactive typed ref whose value is kept in sync with `localStorage[key]`.

a11y.ssr: Storage access is wrapped in try/catch — safe in SSR and private-mode browsers.

string.title: String value
string.desc: Persists a string across page reloads. Reload the page to verify the value is restored.
string.label: Username

object.title: Object value
object.desc: Deep-watches objects and arrays — any nested mutation is persisted automatically. Reload to verify.

sidebar.title: Persisted sidebar state
sidebar.desc: A practical pattern: use useLocalStorage with show-toggle to persist the sidebar's collapsed state across page reloads.

useAutoScroll.title: useAutoScroll
useAutoScroll.description: Auto-scroll helper for drag-and-drop interactions. When the pointer approaches the top or bottom edge of a scrollable container, it automatically scrolls at a speed proportional to the distance from the edge.

seo.metaDescription: useAutoScroll is a Vue 3 composable for drag-and-drop: when the pointer nears the top or bottom edge of a scrollable container, it auto-scrolls by the distance.
seo.intro: useAutoScroll is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable that adds edge auto-scrolling to drag-and-drop interactions — when the pointer approaches the top or bottom edge of a scrollable container it scrolls automatically, faster the closer it gets.

api.handleAutoScroll: Call with the pointer's `clientY` while dragging. Starts/stops auto-scroll based on proximity to container edges.
api.stopAutoScroll: Immediately cancel any ongoing auto-scroll animation.

playground.instruction: Move your pointer near the top or bottom edge of the box below while holding the mouse button to see auto-scroll.

useCalendar.title: useCalendar
useCalendar.description: Composable for building calendar UIs. Generates day grids, handles month/year navigation, supports date ranges, disabled dates, and locale-aware labels.

seo.metaDescription: useCalendar is a Vue 3 composable for calendar UIs: generates day grids, handles month and year navigation, supports date ranges, disabled dates and locale labels.
seo.intro: useCalendar is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable for building calendar UIs — it generates day grids, handles month and year navigation, and supports date ranges, disabled dates and locale-aware labels.

api.weekDayLabels: Array of 7 localized weekday names in the configured format.
api.calendarDays: Array of 35 or 42 `CalendarDay` objects covering the visible grid.
api.navMonth: Decrement / increment the visible month (rolls over year).
api.goToToday: Jump to the current month and year. Returns today's date.
api.goToDate: Jump to the month and year containing the given date.
api.isDateDisabled: Check if a date falls outside min/max bounds or is in the disabled list.

basic.title: Basic calendar
basic.desc: Bind a reactive date to `modelValue` and navigate months.

range.title: With range and constraints
range.body: calendarDays exposes `inRange`, `isRangeStart`, and `isRangeEnd` for styling range selections.

useColorMode.title: useColorMode
useColorMode.description: Reactive singleton for managing light / dark / system color mode. Persists the choice to localStorage, toggles the `.dark` class on the html element, and reacts to OS `prefers-color-scheme` changes.

seo.metaDescription: useColorMode is a Vue 3 composable for light, dark and system color mode: persists to localStorage, toggles the .dark class, reacts to prefers-color-scheme.
seo.intro: useColorMode is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable for managing light, dark and system color mode — a reactive singleton that persists the choice to localStorage, toggles the .dark class, and reacts to OS prefers-color-scheme changes.

props.mode: The raw chosen mode — `'light'`, `'dark'` or `'system'`. Writable: assigning a new value triggers persistence and class application.
props.resolved: Always a concrete `'light'` or `'dark'`. When `mode` is `'system'`, it tracks the OS preference reactively.
props.set: Programmatically change the mode and persist it to localStorage.
props.toggle: Flip between light and dark. If currently `'system'`, it toggles relative to the resolved value.

basic.title: Basic reading
basic.desc: Read `resolved` and `isDark` to adapt UI styling.

toggle.title: Toggle button
toggle.desc: Use `toggle()` to flip between light and dark with a single button.

system.title: Follow OS preference
system.desc: Set `mode` to `'system'` so the app mirrors the user's OS theme choice.

useConfirm.title: useConfirm
useConfirm.description: Imperative confirmation dialogs that return a `Promise(boolean)`. Mount ConfirmDialog once near the root; any component can call `useConfirm().confirm()` to await the user's decision.

seo.metaDescription: useConfirm is a Vue 3 composable for imperative confirmation dialogs that return a Promise. Mount ConfirmDialog once and await the user's decision anywhere.
seo.intro: useConfirm is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable for imperative confirmation dialogs that resolve to a boolean Promise — mount ConfirmDialog once near the root and any component can await the user's decision.

api.confirm: Open a confirmation dialog and return a promise that resolves `true` on confirm and `false` on cancel or dismiss. Calls are queued so only one dialog is visible at a time.

input.title: Heading shown at the top of the dialog.
input.hideCancel: When `true`, removes the cancel button turning the dialog into an alert.
input.dismissable: Allow closing via overlay click or Escape.

playground.title: Confirm action
playground.description: Are you sure you want to proceed?

basic.title: Danger confirm
basic.desc: Destructive operations should use `color: 'danger'` and a clear description.

alert.title: Alert dialog
alert.desc: Use `hideCancel: true` when you only need an acknowledgement, not a decision.

chain.title: Chained confirms
chain.desc: Because `confirm()` returns a Promise, you can chain multiple steps and bail out early on cancel.

useLocale.title: useLocale
useLocale.description: Global reactive locale switcher. All callers share the same state, so changing the locale updates the entire application. Pass the reactive locale object directly to ModoProvider :locale.

seo.metaDescription: useLocale is a Vue 3 composable: a global reactive locale switcher where all callers share state, so changing the locale updates the whole app and ModoProvider.
seo.intro: useLocale is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable for switching locales globally — all callers share the same reactive state, so changing the locale updates the entire app, and the object passes straight to ModoProvider's :locale prop.

api.locale: Reactive `ModoLocale` object — pass directly to ModoProvider :locale.

playground.note: Changing the locale here updates all translated content in the showroom.

provider.title: With ModoProvider
provider.desc: Bind the reactive locale to ModoProvider so child components re-render with translated strings.
provider.body: The locale object is reactive. Pass it to ModoProvider and all descendants react to changes.

useModoConfig.title: useModoConfig
useModoConfig.description: Access the global ModoProvider configuration and helper functions that resolve component props against the global theme. Works inside ModoProvider; falls back to sensible defaults when used outside.

seo.metaDescription: useModoConfig is a Vue 3 composable exposing the global ModoProvider config and the helpers that resolve component props against the active theme.
seo.intro: useModoConfig is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable for reading the global ModoProvider configuration and the helper functions that resolve component props against the active theme — and it falls back to sensible defaults when used outside the provider.

api.config: Returns the full `ModoConfig` object injected by ModoProvider. Returns `undefined` outside a provider.
api.resolvedColor: Resolves a local `color` prop with fallback to global brand color. Semantic values ('primary', 'danger', etc.) always win; 'default' or undefined falls back.
api.resolvedRadius: Resolves radius: local prop → global config → default ('medium').
api.resolvedSize: Resolves size: local prop → global config → default ('medium').
api.resolvedTheme: Resolves theme: local prop → global config → default ('zinc').
api.resolvedHalo: Resolves halo: local prop → global config → default ('tinted').
api.modoLocale: Returns the deep-merged locale object. Works outside ModoProvider too.
api.localeString: Resolves a locale string getter + interpolates `{name}` placeholders.

resolve.title: Resolving props
resolve.desc: When a component prop is 'default' or undefined, use the global theme value.

locale.title: Locale helpers
locale.desc: Access the merged locale and interpolate strings with placeholders.
locale.body: useModoLocale and useLocaleString work even outside a ModoProvider.

useToast.title: useToast
useToast.description: Imperative API to push notifications onto a global toast queue. Mount ToastContainer once near the root; any component can call `useToast()` to fire toasts that appear in the container.

seo.metaDescription: useToast is a Vue 3 composable: an imperative API to push notifications onto a global toast queue. Mount ToastContainer once and fire toasts from any component.
seo.intro: useToast is a free Vue 3 / Nuxt composable that returns an imperative API for pushing notifications onto a global toast queue — mount ToastContainer once near the root and any component can fire toasts that appear in the container.

api.push: Push a toast with full control. Returns an id so you can `dismiss(id)` or re-push to update in place.
api.dismiss: Remove a single toast by the id returned from `push()`.
api.dismissAll: Clear every toast in the queue at once.

basic.title: Basic colors
basic.desc: The four convenience wrappers cover most use cases.

action.title: With action
action.desc: Add an inline action button. The `close` callback dismisses the toast.

pinned.title: Pinned toast
pinned.desc: Set `duration: 0` to keep the toast visible until the user dismisses it.

update.title: Update in place
update.desc: Re-push with the same `id` to replace the toast without creating a new one — perfect for live progress.
